mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers discount rates and eases financial conditions, which supports equity valuation expansion and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is strongest when the policy rate is already above its recent level and fails if rate cuts coincide with recessionary labor-market deterioration.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not positive in a majority of periods when FEDFUNDS is falling on a 3-month basis, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend signals weakening household income and lower corporate revenue expectations, which pushes S&P 500 returns down over the next month. The effect should be visible once unemployment rises materially, not during flat labor-market conditions.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the sign of the average forward return is not negative after UNRATE crosses the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and PAYEMS rise together, the market receives a joint confirmation of stronger consumer demand and employment income, supporting near-term earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns. This is an interaction effect because sales strength without payroll growth, or payroll growth without sales strength, does not provide the same return signal.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined RSAFS-PAYEMS improvement does not outperform either series alone in predicting 20-day returns, or if positive forward returns do not occur in more than 55% of joint-acceleration episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A renewed upturn in INDPRO after a prior soft patch signals improving industrial output and cyclical capacity utilization, which should translate into higher S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The mechanism fails if production improves while demand indicators remain weak enough to imply inventory accumulation rather than real activity.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward S&P 500 returns are not positive on average after two-month INDPRO upturns, or if the signal's rolling 3-year Rank IC falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A high FEDFUNDS level combined with elevated CPIAUCSL indicates restrictive real rates and inflation pressure, which compresses equity multiples and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal requires both a tight policy setting and persistent inflation; either variable alone is insufficient.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL rising on a 3-month basis
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not have negative average 20-day S&P 500 returns, or if positive returns occur in more than 40% of episodes when both conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in PAYEMS and rise in UNRATE signals a genuine labor-market deterioration, which should precede weaker equity returns over the next 20 trading days through lower consumption and earnings expectations. The pattern is falsified if payroll softness is not accompanied by unemployment worsening.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls and UNRATE rises over the same month
failure_condition: Reject if joint deterioration episodes do not produce negative average 20-day S&P 500 returns, or if the sign flips positive in a majority of such episodes.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in GDPC1 signals stronger aggregate final demand and improving revenue growth expectations, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the following quarter. The relationship fails when GDP growth is driven by temporary inventory effects rather than broad demand strength.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth accelerates versus prior quarter
failure_condition: Reject if quarters with accelerating GDPC1 do not lead to positive 60-day S&P 500 returns on average, or if the signal's rolling 3-year Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in HOUST together with softer RSAFS marks a synchronized pullback in housing activity and retail demand, which points to weakening cyclical spending and negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is specific to broad demand contraction and fails when housing weakness is offset by resilient retail sales.
failure_condition: Reject if joint HOUST-RSAFS weakness does not correspond to negative average 20-day S&P 500 returns, or if more than 60% of such episodes are followed by positive returns.
